t_ch: 您这取名是不是差点儿文化？
t_en: You don't think your naming lacks a little sophistication?

t_ch: 二狗二狗，吃喝不愁。二狗二狗，罗浮之友。
t_en: Noob Noob, never want for gulp or slurp. Noob Noob, Luofu's friend, always a perk.

t_ch: 老板要是不喜欢的话，还可以付费解锁「有文化的名字」、「非常文化的名字」…小店服务应有尽有。
t_en: If you don't like that, how about a fee to unlock "Sophisticated Names" and "Seriously Sophisticated Names"... I have services for all needs.

t_ch: 我想卜算一下凶吉祸福。
t_en: I wish to know my fortune.

t_ch: 老板您算是找对人了，这是我们店里的招牌服务「今日运势 」。第一次测试是免费的，我帮您看看……
t_en: You've come to the right person. This is our shop special, the "Fortune for Today." The first try's free. Let me see...

t_ch: 坏了，这是仙舟「苍城」覆灭之景，「抱薪救火大皆燃，烧遍三千亦复然」，老板您最近事不如愿，抱薪救火，不如不救啊。
t_en: Oh dear, this scene paints the destruction of the Xianzhou Cangcheng: "Fighting fire with woods will only fuel the flames." Your recent affairs haven't been going your way, so instead of remedying them, you should leave them be.

t_ch: 小店提供更改运势的服务，<unbreak>199</unbreak>套餐「避凶驱邪」，<unbreak>299</unbreak>套餐「逢凶化吉」，<unbreak>599</unbreak>套餐「以后都只有好事发生」，还挺划算的，老板觉得如何？
t_en: We provide fortune-changing services, like the "Evil Begone" bundle for <unbreak>199</unbreak>, "See No Evil" bundle for <unbreak>299</unbreak>, and the "Only Good Things From Here On Out" bundle for <unbreak>599</unbreak>. All great value! What do you think?

t_ch: 不同套餐的名字好像不太讲究。
t_en: It feels like you didn't really think those bundles' names through.

t_ch: 老板您不必计较这些粗浅的词句，看看价格就知道档位了。
t_en: Those names are of no importance. The prices speak for themselves!

t_ch: 名字嘛，就是包装而已，随便换，随便换。
t_en: Names are just superficial packaging. They can change anytime.

t_ch: 不会是故意帮我求了下下签吧？
t_en: Did you make me pick a dire fortune on purpose?

t_ch: 老板您可错怪我了呀，您若不信我，我们再抽一签。您看，还是一样——「如鸟飞投罗网里，相逢能有几多时」。
t_en: Oh, you hurt me with your words. If you don't believe me, let's try another one. Look, it's still the same: "Like birds into nets, when will reunion be, one frets".

t_ch: 这运势怎么说改就改呢？
t_en: I can change my luck just like that?

t_ch: 运势这种东西也没有那么死板，要是什么都能决定了，岂不是太不自由了吗？
t_en: Your luck isn't something set in stone. If everything was already pre-determined, where would one's freedom be?

t_ch: 今日开门迎客，您恰好是第<unbreak>100</unbreak>位，我送老板您一个「避凶驱邪」套餐，权当交朋友了，这是您今天的「转运饰品」，请拿好。
t_en: You are our lucky <unbreak>100</unbreak>th customer for the day, so I'll give you an "Evil Begone" bundle as a favor. This is your "lucky trinket" for today. Don't lose it now.

t_ch: 老板您还有什么需要帮助的吗？
t_en: Do you have anything else you need help with?

t_ch: 想跟你打听一些事儿…
t_en: I'd like to ask you a few things...

t_ch: 好啊，老板您尽管问，只是要是问了运势，咱可得收费了呀。
t_en: Fire away. But I'll have to start charging if it's to do with your fortune.

t_ch: 你说自己以前是太卜司卜者？
t_en: You said you were once a Divination Commission diviner?

t_ch: 原来老板您是在怀疑我的业务水平啊，不用怀疑，我在太卜司里做了小两百年，被符玄太卜亲自赶出太卜司。
t_en: So, you have doubts about my professional proficiency. No fear, I worked there for almost two centuries, until I was personally ejected by Master Diviner Fu Xuan.

t_ch: 符玄太卜算到了她以后会把我赶出太卜司，于是就把我赶出太卜司了。
t_en: Master Diviner Fu Xuan foresaw that she would remove me from the Divination Commission, so she did.

t_ch: 哪有人会为了未发生之事有着如此笃定的决断呢？就结果而言，我确实离开了太卜司，不过这过程实在是可笑。
t_en: How could someone make a decision based off something that hadn't happened yet? Anyway, I did leave the Commission, but the entire situation was a farce.

t_ch: 太卜司不要我，我还可以在这里开家小小的卜算道具商店。化外民老爷们没法在太卜司里求得预言，大可以来到我这里求签算卦嘛。
t_en: Even if the Divination Commission didn't want me, I could still run my little old divination paraphernalia shop here. Outworlders who can't get their fortunes at the Commission could come to me instead.

t_ch: 我听人说你这里算得不准？
t_en: I've heard that your divinations aren't accurate?

t_ch: 准，非常准，谁说我不准你告诉我。
t_en: Of course they are accurate! Who told you that? Give me their name.

t_ch: …不过嘛，那人也没全部说错，很多时候准和不准纯粹是一种…主观感受。
t_en: ...Then again, they aren't totally wrong. Many times, the line between accuracy and inaccuracy is... subjective.

t_ch: 一般人嘛，想知道的无非就是自己的吉凶祸福，以求对未来有一种掌控感。确实，人身在命运的洪流里，会有个大致的方向。但吉凶之事……
t_en: Most people just want to know about their fortunes and have some sort of control over their future. In truth, everyone moves in a general direction in the great stream of destiny, but when it comes to actual good or ill luck...

t_ch: 那可是谁也无法捉摸的概率暗流。要捕捉如此细微之物，反倒要投入巨量的资源和靠着山一样占卜器械方能成事。
t_en: That's a hidden current of probability no one can ever fathom. To capture such an intricacy, you'd have to invest massive resources and rely on devices as huge as mountains to calculate that.

t_ch: 所以太卜司的规矩「三不卜」里其中一条就是「不卜人事」。这也不是有什么禁忌，实际上就是扰动太甚，得不偿失。
t_en: So, one of the rules in the Divination Commission's "Three Don'ts" is "Don't divine personal matters." It's not particularly taboo or anything — just that there are too many variables and does more harm than good.

t_ch: 不过我嘛，我有另外的法子。我会尽量用模糊的方式告诉客户占卜结果——反正敝小店也只能算些模糊的走向。
t_en: But for me? I've got other ways. I provide clients with the most ambiguous results possible — it's about the limit of accuracy for my tiny shop anyway.

t_ch: 我看这里还有些牌桌？
t_en: I see a few game tables here as well?

t_ch: 做生意嘛，做占卜也是赚钱，开棋牌馆也是赚钱，不冲突。
t_en: I do business. Divination makes money, and so do games of cards and tiles. There's no conflict of interest.

t_ch: 之前在太卜司的时候认识了一位小姑娘叫作青雀，你要是经常来这里，也会经常看到她。她从书库里复原了这个棋牌戏，就叫「帝垣琼玉」。
t_en: I knew a girl called Qingque during my time at the Divination Commission. You'll run into her if you come here often. She recreated this game called "Celestial Jade" from old books.

t_ch: 三五朋友聚在一起玩玩牌，多好呀，看着他们玩儿，我心情也好起来了。要是开个棋牌馆就能赚钱，我就不搞收费占卜了。
t_en: How nice it'd be to play a few rounds with friends. It lifts my spirits just seeing them play, too. If I could earn a living with a games parlor, I wouldn't be doing any divination.

t_ch: 没有其他想问的事儿了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 老板您下次再来，要是运势不好，小店是可以帮您转运的。
t_en: Come back again, esteemed customer. If you're down on your luck, I'll be here to help you turn it around.

t_ch: 没有其他想问的事儿了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 老板您下次再来，要是运势不好，小店是可以帮您转运的。
t_en: Come back again, esteemed customer. If you're down on your luck, I'll be here to help you turn it around.

t_ch: 哟，这位老板您又来啦？稍等一下，我在看闲书呢，讲的是「预言的自我实现」。
t_en: Oh, you're back again? Hold on, I'm reading a book called "A Prophecy's Self-fulfillment."

t_ch: 真没意思，还是跟老板您聊天有意思。今天是要测运势呢，还是要闲聊呢？
t_en: Well, that was boring. It's much more interesting to talk to you. Are you here to determine your luck, or just to chat?

t_ch: 就好比，我都不必掐指起卦，便可算到客人少则一周内，多则月余会有一笔小财进账。你说准不准？准得很呐。
t_en: For example, I don't even have to pinch my fingers to foretell that you will have a small amount of wealth coming in sometime between this week and this month. Am I right? Of course I am.

t_ch: 就好比，我都不必掐指起卦，便可算到客人少则一周内，多则月余会有一笔小财进账。你说准不准？准得很呐。
t_en: For example, I don't even have to pinch my fingers to foretell that you will have a small amount of wealth coming in sometime between this week and this month. Am I right? Of course I am.

t_ch: 建木又长起来了…
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor is growing again...

t_ch: 嗐，老板，这可不是个什么好兆头啊。
t_en: Tsk, that can't be a good omen.

t_ch: 这几日罗浮仙舟祸乱频出，大家相信云骑军能够处理好大大小小许多事情。
t_en: The Luofu has been ravaged by disaster recently. Everyone thinks that the Cloud Knights are capable of solving every problem under the sun, no matter how great or small.

t_ch: 可是这建木长成就不是云骑军能够掌控的范畴了……
t_en: But the Ambrosial Arbor's growth doesn't fall within the scope of operations for the Cloud Knights...

t_ch: 现如今这情况，不知道太卜大人算到了吗？嗐，多半是没有算到吧。
t_en: I'm not sure if the master diviner has foreseen this? I'm thinking, probably not.

t_ch: 你有给建木这事算过吗？
t_en: Have you divined for the Ambrosial Arbor crisis?

t_ch: 当初建木生发的时候我起过一卦，现在我倒没再看过了。我觉得也不必再看了，没什么好惊奇的，既有生发，就必然会停止。
t_en: I divined when it sprouted, but I didn't bother to check again later because I didn't think it was necessary. Neither its sprouting or its sudden stop came as a surprise to me.

t_ch: 就像…就像卦有两爻，阴阳二爻一体两面，建木会突然生长，就会突然停止，无论是否有其他变数，落在建木的具体表现上就只有这两种结果。
t_en: It's just like... like each sign consists of yin and yang. If the Ambrosial Arbor suddenly sprouts, then it's destined to stop just as suddenly.. This result is bound to happen.

t_ch: 嗐，至于那些什么影响啊，风波啊，那就是太卜大人他们的职责了。我又没公职在身，何必瞎操心，还是研究下晚饭吃什么更重要吧。
t_en: As for the impact, I'm not an official, and I don't think it's my job to worry about it. That's what the master diviner is for, right? For me, I'm more interested in figuring out what I'm going to eat for dinner.

t_ch: 这位客官，不好意思打扰你一下，看您的样子应该是化外民游客，有没有什么特别的食材、做法能告诉我吗？
t_en: Hello, guest. Sorry to bother you, but I noticed from your attire that you are an Outworld traveler. Do you have any unique ingredients or recipes that you can tell me about?

t_ch: 我自己是不做饭的。
t_en: I don't do my own cooking.

t_ch: 原来如此，这可真是不好意思呀。
t_en: I see. Sorry for bothering you then.

t_ch: 都是些家常菜，没有特别的。
t_en: It's all home-cooked fare. Nothing special.

t_ch: 这样吗？都怪我我问得有些奇怪了。
t_en: Is that so? I guess I'm the one asking a strange question.

t_ch: 星际蝗虫听说过吗？
t_en: Have you heard of interstellar locusts?

t_ch: 吃过…感觉还不太够味儿，谢谢你啊。
t_en: I've eaten them before... They didn't taste great though. Thanks anyways.

t_ch: 哎呀，忘了自我介绍……咱是尚滋味的主厨兼老板燕翠。现在餐厅里不太忙，我到这长乐天里休息休息，结果脑子还在想着怎么开发新的菜式。
t_en: Ah, I forgot to introduce myself... I am Yancui, head chef and owner of Spices Supreme. The restaurant's not busy right now, so I'm taking a break here in Exalting Sanctum, but couldn't take my mind off developing new dishes.

t_ch: 咱们尚滋味就在附近，您要是感兴趣就到店里坐坐。咱们尚滋味的立命之本就是「亲民」，保准道道菜都便宜又好吃！
t_en: Spices Supreme isn't far from here. Please visit us if you're interested. Our motto is to "cook for the people." We guarantee our food is inexpensive and tasty!

t_ch: 哎，一不小心和您说了这么多。这个送给你尝尝吧，以后有机会一定要来尝尝咱们尚滋味哦，包您终身难忘！
t_en: Ah, I've chatted so much with you. Here's something for you to have a taste test. You must come to Spices Supreme to try our dishes if you get a chance! It'll be an experience you'll never forget!

t_ch: 怎么？打算和咱再探讨一下美食吗？咱最喜欢和人一起研究美食了。
t_en: Hmm? You want to discuss food for a little longer? I love to chat about cuisine with people.

t_ch: 有点儿事想问你…
t_en: There is something I want to ask you...

t_ch: 好啊，您尽管问。只要不涉及商业机密，咱肯定知无不言。
t_en: Go ahead. I'll tell you everything I know, as long as they're not business secrets.

t_ch: 商业机密是指什么？
t_en: Business secrets? Such as?

t_ch: 嗯？咱就随口那么一说，还真未见得有什么商业机密……
t_en: Hmm? I was just saying that! I don't think I have anything you'd call a business secret...

t_ch: 说来也不怕您笑话，我们仙舟的厨子们都很忌讳将自己做菜的秘诀公之于众，甚至将一些很普通的技巧神秘化……
t_en: You might laugh at us, but we Xianzhou chefs are very careful about our recipes being leaked to the public. Some of our most common cooking methods have even been mystified...

t_ch: 就比如说吧，把嫩豆腐切成蚕丝般的细线…这玩意儿说到底就是下苦功夫、笨功夫，勤学苦练而已。
t_en: For example, slicing tofu into thin threads as fine as silk... At the end of the day, it's just putting in the time and effort and learning through mistakes.

t_ch: 到了某些厨子的嘴里，就成了「腕子劲儿」、「巧功夫」、「不传之秘」这些让人越听越糊涂的大词儿…咱听着就觉得奇怪。
t_en: But spoken from a chef's perspective, it becomes all about the "power in the wrists," "artful skill," "unfathomable secret" — all these pretentious big words that confuse people... Even I'm left scratching my head.

t_ch: 尚滋味决不搞这一套，您要真想知道某道菜是怎么做的，咱都可以细细讲给您听。一次学不会没关系，搜索「尚滋味燕翠」，那有咱的教学视频。
t_en: Spices Supreme employs no such tactics. If you really want to know how a dish is made, I'll even give you a step-by-step. Doesn't matter if you don't get it the first time: Just look up "Spices Supreme Yancui" online and my recipes' videos are all there.

t_ch: 有什么推荐菜吗？
t_en: Do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 您可以先试试脆瓜胡辣鸡丁、鲫香肉丝和陈婆豆腐这些招牌菜。这些都是历经时间考验的名菜，点这些准不会错。
t_en: You can try our trademark dishes like the crisped melon and diced chicken, pork slices in fish sauce, and Granny Chen's Tofu to start. These are all famous dishes that have withstood the test of time. You can't go wrong with them.

t_ch: 有不过口味这东西十人十色，咱的推荐未必符合您的胃口。但是咱们尚滋味有千种滋味，总有一款属于您的菜。
t_en: Everyone's tastes are different, and my recommendations might not suit your palate. But we've got myriads of flavors, so I'm sure there's one that'll pique your fancy.

t_ch: 您也知道，仙舟人的一生很漫长，所以特别追求各种不同的味觉体验。
t_en: You know that a Xianzhou native's life is long, so they are very particular about variety in their pursuit of flavor.

t_ch: 每周四，咱这儿会搞一个「怪味星期四」，出一些特殊口味的「怪味菜」。这也是为了给仙舟的老饕们增加一些变数和乐趣。
t_en: Every Thursday, we've got an "Odd-taste Thursday" event, where we serve some dishes with rather interesting flavors. It's to provide some variety and fun for Xianzhou gormandizers.

t_ch: 这些新推出的怪味菜未必会好吃，但是一定可以给您带来难以忘记的全新体验。
t_en: These new dishes may not be tasty, but they'll definitely leave you with an unforgettable experience.

t_ch: 你自己最喜欢什么菜？
t_en: What's your favorite dish?

t_ch: 说来惭愧，虽然咱是开饭馆儿的，但其实比较挑食。有一些东西我是不吃的…比方说……
t_en: I'm ashamed to admit that though I run a restaurant, I'm actually quite picky about my food. There are some things I don't eat... such as...

t_ch: 没办法打败咱的动物，咱是坚决不吃的。
t_en: Such as animals who can't defeat me — I'll never eat those!

t_ch: 咱们持明一族历来讲究一个道法自然。万物相生相克、生长不息。所以基于这种原理，如果一个动物无法与咱公平对决，咱也不会以它为食！这就是咱的武道！
t_en: We Vidyadhara have always observed the law of natural flow. All creatures are connected to one another, and life is everlasting in its cycle. Based on this principle, if an animal can't defeat me on even ground, I'd never use it as food! That is my martial way!

t_ch: 在说什么怪话…
t_en: What exactly are you talking about...

t_ch: 这前后逻辑是？
t_en: What's the logic behind this?

t_ch: 嗐！这就是您不懂了啊！别看咱细胳膊细腿儿，咱可是「云曳旗旌流星拳」的首席大弟子。咱这路拳法可堪称仙舟外家拳之王！
t_en: C'mon, that's where you're ignorant! I may look like a pushover, but I am the Cloudbanner Meteor Fist's chief disciple. My fists are unrivaled among the auxiliary martial art schools in Xianzhou!

t_ch: 只不过嘛，咱后来还是去做了餐饮业，没继承武馆，但终归还是留下了不少武人的傲骨呀，嘿嘿。
t_en: It's just that I went into the food business in the end, so I didn't take over the martial art school. But I've still retained the pride of a martial artist, heehee.

t_ch: 咱从不吃那些小鱼小虾，也不吃食草动物，更不吃被驯化的猛兽…咱只吃有资格与咱生死对决的动物。
t_en: I don't eat small fish and shrimp, nor herbivores, or even domesticated beasts... I only eat animals that I can fight to the death with.

t_ch: 当然啦，这只是咱个人的坚持。咱们尚滋味还是什么食材都肯提供的哈，请您不必担心。
t_en: Of course, that's just my personal mantra. Spices Supreme serves everything. Don't worry.

t_ch: 所以…你最喜欢什么菜来着？
t_en: So... what's your favorite dish?

t_ch: 扯远了，都忘了这茬了！咱们持明的人口不会自然增长，所以天舶司那边的外派审查也相对宽松一些，咱经常有机会去其他世界进行狩猎。
t_en: Oh, I completely forgot your question! We Vidyadhara can't reproduce naturally, so the Sky-Faring Commission's travel reviewers are fairly lenient, which means I've had many opportunities to hunt on other worlds.

t_ch: 有些世界栖息着无数猛兽…我印象最深的是一颗叫塔拉萨的海洋世界……
t_en: Some worlds teem with numerous ferocious beasts... My most vivid memory was of an ocean world called Thalassa...

t_ch: 那里有一种巨型的浮空兽，叫做「六眼飞鱼」。咱当时骑在它身上，足足鏖战了两三个时辰，好悬没让它给咱囫囵个儿吞下去！
t_en: It had a giant airborne beast called a six-eyed flying fish. I stood on it, and we were locked in battle for hours. It was a miracle it didn't swallow me whole!

t_ch: 「六眼飞鱼」的那个鱼翅，可别提多好吃了！那一份鱼翅煲，是咱平生吃过最好吃的……
t_en: The fins of that fish were delicious beyond compare! That fin stew was the tastiest dish I'd ever had...

t_ch: 除此之外，咱还喜欢油爆飞海葵、果木炙烤夏塔恩恐鸟翅根、视肉糖球炒腊肉…哦对，这个视肉虽然个体极小，但会造出可怕的幻觉，极难对付……
t_en: Apart from that, I also like deep-fried flying sea anemone, fruitwood-grilled Shantak moa drumettes, viscorpus sugar balls stir-fried with cured meat... Oh right, these viscorpi may be really tiny, but they sure can conjure horrifying hallucinations. What a ride...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，不好意思，很少有人问我这些取材经历，不小心讲到兴头上了。
t_en: Haha, sorry, very few people ask me about my ingredient-seeking escapades, and I got carried away.

t_ch: 是…是吗…真厉害呀…
t_en: Is... Is that so...? Amazing...

t_ch: 哼哼，咱确实很厉害呀。不仅武功高强，做的菜也很好吃，客官一定要试试看哦！
t_en: Heheh, I am quite amazing! Not just at martial arts, but cooking too. You must try my food out!

t_ch: 关于尚滋味…
t_en: About Spices Supreme...

t_ch: 您是想问咱为什么要开这么一家店吗？哎，这可就说来话长了……
t_en: You're wondering why I opened this restaurant? Well, it's a long story...

t_ch: 大概一百年前吧，星际和平公司有个卖陆地履带的子公司，搞了个什么「星际美食家协会」。他们就在银河内，给各种餐厅评定星级。
t_en: The IPC had this subsidiary company a hundred years ago which sold conveyor belts, and they had an "Interastral Gourmet Association" that went around and rated all the eateries throughout the whole cosmos.

t_ch: 一星就算是了不起的「星际严选」了，五星则会被称为「银河最佳餐厅」。整个仙舟世界唯一拿到他们五星评级的是朱明仙舟上的一家「龙宫私房菜」。
t_en: If you get one star, you're part of the "Interastral Selection." If you get five stars, then you're the "Cosmos's Finest Diner." Across all of the Xianzhou ships, the only place to ever get five stars from them is "Dragon Palace's Kitchen" on the Xianzhou Zhuming.

t_ch: 咱也是持明嘛，心想着持明同胞得到了这样的荣誉，咱高低得去捧捧场…结果去了一看，您敢信吗？喂鸟都不够吃的一点东西，他们竟敢要价几十万信用点！
t_en: Being Vidyadhara, I thought I had to go and support the business of my kin, especially when they received such honors... But that place charged hundreds of thousands of credits for portions that won't even be filling for little birds! Would you believe it?

t_ch: 那个主厨还在那里装腔作势地和来吃饭的化外民说：「我们持明生性淡泊名利，就爱吃这一口儿。」哎，真是一言难尽……
t_en: That chef even put on this pretentious air and told Outworlders, "We Vidyadhara are indifferent to the trappings of fame and fortune, which is why we eat like this." Ack, that was too much...

t_ch: 从此之后，咱就立志要开一家与他们背道而驰的饭馆：量大管饱、物美价廉！咱们这家尚滋味，就是这个梦想的结晶啦！
t_en: That's when I determined to run a restaurant that goes completely opposite to everything they stood for. I want big portions, quality food, and low prices! I'm telling ya, Spices Supreme is that dream come true!

t_ch: 不过说实在话，您今天要是不提，咱都快忘了这些渊源了…现在咱做饭开店，就图一乐儿，别无他念。
t_en: But to be honest, if you hadn't mentioned it today, I'd have forgotten about that goal... Now I run the restaurant just to have fun. Nothing else.

t_ch: 下次再来吃吧…
t_en: I'll come back later...

t_ch: 行，都行。您啥时候来都行，咱翘首以盼！慢走啊，路上小心！
t_en: Fine. Not to worry. Any time! Can't wait! Take care! Stay safe out there!

t_ch: 这次「创意星期四」应该推出哪些菜品呢…咱得看看…大肠刺身、腰子炖汤、红糖芝麻酱、清蒸野牛皮、炭烤黑驴蹄、拔丝臭豆腐……
t_en: Let's have a look then. What shall we have for this week's Odd-taste Thursday... Gizzard sashimi, kidney stew, brown sugar sesame porridge, steamed boar skin, charcoal barbecued black donkey hooves, caramel-drizzled stinky tofu...

t_ch: 建木又长起来了…
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor is growing again...

t_ch: 当时还在后厨里，咱只听到饭店里的食客们喧哗大作。
t_en: I was still working back of house then, and all we could hear was the clattering of diners out front.

t_ch: 咱急急忙忙从厨房里出来，看着那建木缓缓地长起来了。我也算是见过世面了，但也着实是吃了一惊。
t_en: I rushed out of the kitchen and watched as the Arbor slowly grew. I had been pretty well-traveled, but I was flabbergasted.

t_ch: 咱光是看着那个东西，就觉得有些毛骨悚然。它不疾不徐地生长起来，从容地仿佛天地之间只有它一棵树一样……
t_en: Just looking at that thing made my skin crawl. It grew at this weird, steady pace as if it were the only tree in all of existence...

t_ch: 练了这么多年拳法，咱在这方面直觉还是很准确的…那东西带有过于强大的生命力，几乎可以摧枯拉朽……
t_en: After all these years of boxing, my intuition is still dead-on about this kind of thing... That thing is so packed full of vitality that it could almost raise the dead...

t_ch: 所以…您之后也要小心行事啊，我还等着您来咱的店里惠顾呢。
t_en: So... You have to be careful in the future. I'm still waiting for you to come and visit us in-store.

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 承蒙您关照，咱家店最近生意不错。前几天还上了一个化外民的什么「银河百大黑暗菜肴榜」…哈哈。
t_en: Thanks to your sponsorship, my business has been going well. A few days ago, my store was even ranked on the "Top <unbreak>100</unbreak> Darkest Dishes in the Cosmos" made by Outworlders... Haha.

t_ch: 好多人都怕咱生气，不敢跟咱提这茬…其实这有啥的，我高兴还来不及呢！
t_en: My friends were afraid to tell me about it because they thought I'd get angry.... but I'm actually happy!

t_ch: 最近每天都有不少化外民专程赶过来，要尝尝咱这「黑暗菜肴」，还有个「黑暗菜肴评议会」要吸纳咱当会员呢！
t_en: Recently, I've been getting a lot of attention from Outworlders who come here just to try my "dark dishes." They even formed a "Dark Cuisine Council" and have been asking me to join as a member!

t_ch: 前者咱当然乐见，但后者嘛…咱这一世最讨厌的就是厨师抱团搞什么行业规范，所以就敬谢不敏啦。
t_en: I'm glad to have more customers. As for the "Council," however... I hate the idea of chefs grouping together to come up with "industry standards" or something like that, so I've refused their offer.

t_ch: 有空记得来咱这儿好好吃一顿啊…无论这尚滋味有多忙，只要您来，就一定有座位。
t_en: Just drop by and enjoy a hearty meal whenever you're free. My store might be busy these days, but I'll always reserve a seat for you!

t_ch: 建木的事总算过去了…
t_en: Finally, the Ambrosial Arbor crisis is behind us...

t_ch: 是啊，总算过去了…其实算算时间也没多久，但总有种熬了很久的错觉。
t_en: Yeah, it's finally over... Even though it hasn't been that long, it feels like we've been going through it for a long time.

t_ch: 咱现在还记清清楚楚呢，当时从后厨出来看到建木长出来的画面…建木不再生长之后倒再没出过这么大的动静了。
t_en: I still remember clearly that scene, when I was in my kitchen and I came out and saw it growing... I've never seen anything so spectacular since it stopped growing.

t_ch: 这样也好…吃饭就该有吃饭的样子，前些日子大家都乱了心思，来吃饭的人几乎人人心事重重，这碗里的菜肴都没了味道。
t_en: This is actually a good thing... A restaurant should feel like a restaurant. Back then, everyone was so troubled and weighed down by their worries that they couldn't even fully enjoy the dishes I had carefully prepared for them.

t_ch: …这样不好，这样不好…要咱说还是现在这样，大家都安生了才好。看大家吃得香，咱在后厨料理食材才有劲儿。
t_en: ...That was definitely a terrible time... But now, everyone has calmed down, and I feel happy and motivated to see how they enjoy my dishes.

t_ch: 玩具、门铃、透金镜～展阅、冰箱、油烟机～星槎、玉兆、按摩椅～二手、修理、高价收～
t_en: Toys, doorbells, flexible displays!~ Shelves, refrigerators, extractor fans!~ Starskiffs, jade abaci, massage chairs!~ Second hand, or broken, I buy them all!

t_ch: 唱什么歌呢？
t_en: What song are you singing?

t_ch: …唱歌？哦哦…我这哪是唱歌啊，我这是吆喝！
t_en: ...Singing? Oh... I'm not singing. I'm hawking for business!

t_ch: （拍手打节奏）
t_en: (Clapping in rhythm)

t_ch: 玩具、门铃…停停停，你别光顾着拍手了，我这只是卖吆喝。您这一拍手，反倒是变成街头节目了。
t_en: Toys, doorbells... Wait, don't just clap along. I'm hawking for business. If you clap like that, people will think it's a street show.

t_ch: 哦对了，我是陈机铺的掌柜，连烟。说是掌柜，其实店里也就我一个人，小本经营。
t_en: Oh yeah, by the way — I'm Lianyan, manager at the Oldies Depot. And when I say manager, I mean I'm the only person there. It's just me and my small business.

t_ch: 我这家陈机铺主营的业务，主要是贩卖二手货、修理旧物还有收购古董。但是仙舟人嘛，一个个都活得老长了，对旧物没什么兴趣，也不觉得有什么值得修理……
t_en: The Oldies Depot mainly buys and sells second-hand appliances, fixes up old stuff, and buys antiques. But Xianzhou people live so long that they have no interest in anything old, let alone bothering to fix anything...

t_ch: 收破烂的？
t_en: So you buy trashed items?

t_ch: …这只是个称呼，您爱怎么叫就怎么叫。
t_en: ...It's just a title. Call it whatever you want.

t_ch: 仙舟人喜新厌旧？
t_en: Do the Xianzhou people only like new things?

t_ch: 话虽不能这么说…但我那家店大部分主顾，还都是化外民。偶尔有仙舟人来，也就是贩卖一些废品赚点小钱，没有人来买旧物或者修东西的。
t_en: Not quite... But most of the regulars here are Outworlders. Sometimes we'll get a Xianzhou shopper selling some junk to make a little pocket money, but never anyone buying second-hand goods or repairing things.

t_ch: 我看你也没有带着什么旧物来，怎么，难不成是跟我聊天来了？
t_en: You don't seem to have anything old there that needs fixing. Don't tell me you've come for a chat?

t_ch: 只是想和你聊聊天…
t_en: That's right. I just want to chat...

t_ch: 还真是和我聊天？你可真有意思…你想聊什么？
t_en: Really? How curious... What do you want to chat about?

t_ch: 陈机铺是什么？
t_en: What's the Oldies Depot about?

t_ch: 陈机铺以前是我师父经营的门店。这些年他临近蜕生，便将这些差事一五一十全部交付于我。其实我一开始也是丝毫不感兴趣的。
t_en: My teacher used to run it, but he handed over everything to me since it was almost time for him to undergo his hatching rebirth. I never cared for this place in the slightest, to begin with.

t_ch: 师父以前就是个特别严肃、少言寡语的人，只有在处理旧物的时候，偶尔会露出笑容。对他来说，旧物换新颜是件了不得的事情。
t_en: My master used to be so darn strict. A man of very few words, only smiling when he was handling antique items. If you ask him, renovating something old is the most amazing pursuit there could ever be.

t_ch: 然而仙舟人的生命像死水一样漫长，但仙舟上的一切物品却又在高速地新陈代谢，就像死水里面朝生暮死的小虫。
t_en: But these Xianzhou people live on and on, just like stagnant water. Yet all the stuff aboard Xianzhou metabolizes super-fast — like bugs in that stagnant water, flailing about fleetingly.

t_ch: 他总是讲「天不生无用之人，地不造无用之物。」简单来说就是，没有任何一样事物与人是真正的废弃物。
t_en: He always said, "Heaven makes no useless people, and the world makes no useless things." Meaning that, nothing and nobody is ever truly a waste.

t_ch: 所以这家小小陈机铺，就是给人心和旧物的一点自留地。总有一些难以替代的东西需要这样的旧物翻新的服务。
t_en: So this little Oldies Depot is a place for people's keepsakes and old things. There's always gonna be something irreplaceable that needs fixing up.

t_ch: 仙舟人为什么不喜欢修理旧物？
t_en: Why don't the Xianzhou people like to repair old things?

t_ch: 仙舟上很少有传家宝…或者说，传家宝大多不是什么常用的物件。因为常用的物件寿命总是比仙舟人短很多。
t_en: There are hardly any heirlooms aboard Xianzhou... Or to put it another way, heirlooms aren't everyday items. Items used every day would never keep up with the long-lived Xianzhou people.

t_ch: 抛开技术日新月异的新产品不谈，即使是些锅碗瓢盆的寻常物件，它们很少有能够长存的，修修补补也都是徒劳。
t_en: Forget about all the hot new tech that comes out every week. Even mundane stuff like pots and pans rarely last forever. Fixing things takes effort.

t_ch: 而这些「坏掉的程度」往往需要卓越的技巧以及高昂的价格…简而言之，修理并不划算，购买新的更加便宜也更加方便。
t_en: And their state of disrepair often requires great skill and a high price to get it fixed... Simply put, repairs are not cheap, and buying something new works out cheaper and more convenient.

t_ch: 于是在化外民眼中，有着几百年历史的「古董」就这样随便丢给了「收破烂」的，确实挺令人惊讶的。
t_en: That's why it raises eyebrows whenever Outworlders see an antique from centuries ago get discarded without a thought, and being taken in by us as "trash."

t_ch: 你为什么要做这样的营生？
t_en: What made you take up this line of business?

t_ch: 你喜欢旅游吗？我挺喜欢旅游的。在办了一大堆的破手续后，我去过一个仙舟外的旅游胜地，叫伊须磨洲。
t_en: Do you like traveling? I do. I got through all the paperwork and went to this tourist resort off Xianzhou called Insumousu.

t_ch: 伊须磨洲的人们有一个特性，出生后直到青年时期都是陆生动物，用肺呼吸。但成年后就会变成水栖动物，用腮呼吸。
t_en: The people of Insumousu are special indeed. From their birth until their teenage years, they live on land and breathe with their lungs. But once they reach adulthood, they start to live in water and breathe using gills.

t_ch: 在文明尚未完全开化的铁器时代，伊须磨洲人在海底建立了壮观的建筑群。在遗迹的中心有一座巨大的造像，从海底直冲云霄。
t_en: Back during the Iron Age, when civilization had barely begun, the people of Insumousu constructed magnificent buildings on the sea floor. Right in the middle of them is a huge statue, rising up out of the sea and stretching into the sky.

t_ch: 我遇到的每个伊须磨洲人谈起那片伟大的遗迹时，都两眼冒光，说那是他们文明的图腾，记录着一个伟大民族的伟大历程……
t_en: Every Insumousu local I met lit up in excitement whenever they mentioned that great ruin. They tell me how it's a totem for their civilization, one that records the noble histories of their great people...

t_ch: 后来我才知道，那片遗迹只有四百多年的历史。
t_en: I later found out that the ruins were actually just four hundred years old.

t_ch: 换言之，伊须磨洲伟大的先民们修建「文明的图腾」所消耗的时间，远没有我的人生经历长。
t_en: In other words, all the time spent by the great ancestors of Insumousu to build this "totem of civilization" was far shorter than my entire life.

t_ch: …我并不是在贬低伊须磨洲的文明历程，伊须磨洲的海底遗迹，真得特别美，特别壮丽…它比我年轻，却比我的整个人生都要厚重。
t_en: ...I don't mean to talk down on the Insumousu civilization. Their underwater ruins are exquisite and truly magnificent... It's something that is both younger than me, yet has far more gravity than my life.

t_ch: 没别的事了，你忙吧。
t_en: That's all. I can see you're busy.

t_ch: 好嘞，下回再见。以后要是有什么修旧物、买二手、卖废品的需求记得来找我。
t_en: Alright, catch you next time then. If you ever need anything fixed, or if you want to buy and sell second-hand stuff, come find me.

t_ch: 玩具、门铃、透金镜～星槎、玉兆、按摩椅～二手、修理、高价收～
t_en: Toys, doorbells, mirrors!~ Starskiffs, jade abaci, massage chairs!~ Second hand, or broken, I buy them all!

t_ch: 哦，你又来啦。要修理旧物，还是要随便聊聊呢？
t_en: Oh hey, you're back. Got something for me to fix? Or just a casual catch-up?

t_ch: 建木又长起来了…
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor is growing again...

t_ch: 看到了，之前我还到鳞渊境附近凑热闹来着，被云骑给赶回来了，哈哈。
t_en: I saw, and even tried going to nearby Scalegorge Waterscape to rubberneck, but the Cloud Knights sent me back, haha.

t_ch: 其实建木生发不是件好事，但…咱们私下里偷偷说…我特别喜欢建木的外观。
t_en: The Arbor's growth isn't actually a good thing... But let's talk somewhere more private... I love how beautiful the Ambrosial Arbor looks.

t_ch: 仅限于外观哦！我也是熟悉仙舟历史的，这可不是什么好东西。
t_en: But only how it looks! I've studied my history and this is not a good thing.

t_ch: 但也没有办法，我可能天生就是喜欢这种雄伟壮丽的景观。
t_en: But I can't help it. I guess I was just born with a love for majestic landscapes like this.

t_ch: 建木不再生长了…
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor has stopped growing...

t_ch: 我早知道了。我一听说建木的风波平息下来了，马上就去凑了凑热闹，毕竟眼见为实嘛。从长乐天那里不就能望见建木吗，我看到了。
t_en: Yeah, that's hardly news to me. As soon as I heard about it, I went there and checked it out myself, and I saw from Exalting Sanctum with my own eyes that it had stopped growing.

t_ch: 咳咳…有一件事告诉了您，您可千万别跟人说啊，免得害我到地衡司走一遭…先前有些人卖旧物的时候故意往「建木生发」的事上蹭呢。
t_en: Well... I'll tell you a secret, but don't tell anyone, okay? Otherwise I might end up in hot water with the Realm-Keeping Commission... Some curio sellers were using the Ambrosial Arbor's sprouting as a gimmick to attract customers.

t_ch: 一会儿是受了什么异变啊，一会儿又什么建木的落叶啊，我跟你说都是骗人的，你在外面要是见着了也别上当。
t_en: Just so you know, they were claiming that their curios were influenced by the anomaly or even came from the Ambrosial Arbor itself. But don't fall for their tricks, okay?

t_ch: 眼下建木不再生长了，我倒讨了个巧，把这些稀奇玩意儿的价格给压下来了，抛开瞎编的故事不说，有些物什还挺有意思的，算是个意外之喜吧。
t_en: Now that the Ambrosial Arbor has stopped growing, I've seen an opportunity and acquired those curios at incredibly low prices. To my surprise, some of them are actually pretty interesting and not just cheap gimmicks like I thought they would be. A pleasant surprise, don't you agree?

t_ch: 最近生意还顺遂吗？
t_en: Has business been going well lately?

t_ch: 最近生意好得不得了啊！以前金人巷没什么游客，我做的也都是仙舟人的生意…但仙舟人嘛，你也懂，不太需要我的业务。
t_en: It's been amazing! Aurum Alley never used to have any travelers, and I only ever did business with Xianzhou locals... But, well, you know how the Xianzhou folks are like... They don't really need my services.

t_ch: 现在今非昔比啦！金人巷到处都是化外民，他们对仙舟那些成百上千年历史的「旧物」可感兴趣了。
t_en: But everything has changed, now! The Aurum Alley is full of outworlders and they love the ancient "artifacts" of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 你们化外民对旧物的价值判断和我们不同，所以周围很多朋友都暗搓搓劝我提高些价格…可旧物就是旧物嘛。
t_en: These outworlders value artifacts differently to us, so my friends keep telling me to up the prices but... well, it's just old stuff, isn't it?

t_ch: 我这辈子也没想过发大财，只有一个简单的愿望——「物尽其用」。仅此而已。
t_en: I've never dreamed of becoming rich in this life. I have just one simple wish: "to make the best use of everything." That is all.

t_ch: …这里是书店，要是想看书、选书，直接进店翻阅就好，要结账的时候再来叫我吧。
t_en: ...This is a bookstore. If you want to read or pick out a book, just go in and browse around. Call me when you want to pay.

t_ch: 有没有什么书推荐呢？
t_en: Got anything you can recommend?

t_ch: 我读书不多，可能很难回答你…这样吧，店内前排放着的都是些畅销书，他们成为畅销书肯定是有原因的，你看这些如何？
t_en: I don't really read that much, so I can't really recommend anything... Well, the front of the shop is where we keep our bestsellers. There's gotta be a reason why they're bestsellers. Why not start there?

t_ch: 《涯海星槎胜览》，狐族冒险家撰写的异星奇观记录…哦，你本来就是来自异星的游客。稍等，我想想……
t_en: "Views of the Universe from a Starskiff" is a record of wondrous exoplanets by a foxian explorer... Oh, you're from an exoplanet anyways. In that case, hang on and let me think...

t_ch: 这本《厌作人间语》如何？这是小店老板写的志怪小说集子。
t_en: How about this one here, "Otherworldly Delights"? It's a collection of short supernatural stories written by the boss here.

t_ch: 老板，你的态度好冷淡。
t_en: Boss, you could be a little more enthusiastic.

t_ch: …书店本来就是这样的销售方式嘛。再说了，我也不是书肆的老板，我只是帮老板看店的杂务，不为书店的销量负责。
t_en: ...Bookstores are for selling books. Besides, I'm not the boss here. I just help with running a few chores. I'm not in charge of sales here.

t_ch: 话又说回来，那位老板其实也不在意书店生意。关于老板…你看，这是他写的书，叫作《厌作人间语》，是本志怪小说集子。
t_en: But then again, the boss here doesn't really give a darn about business at all. Here... This is a collection of short supernatural stories he's written, called "Otherworldly Delights."

t_ch: 书肆老板是我的亲戚，他喜好听人闲聊，说是为了收集灵感，用来作为写书的点子。
t_en: I'm a relative of the owner. He likes listening to other people's conversations, saying that it's good inspiration as an author.

t_ch: 你可能在星槎海见过，他在一家叫「不夜侯」的茶馆里说书，自称什么「西衍先生」。
t_en: You may have seen him over in Starskiff Haven. He does storytelling in some teahouse called Sleepless Earl or other, calling himself something like "Mr. Xiyan."

t_ch: 我好像在星槎海见过。
t_en: I may have met him in Starskiff Haven.

t_ch: 那个人还挺喜欢引人瞩目的，你要是没见到过，我反倒是会奇怪。
t_en: He likes getting attention. I'd be truly surprised if you haven't seen him.

t_ch: 怎么仙舟还有人说书啊？
t_en: Why are there storytellers aboard Xianzhou?

t_ch: 你在别的世界也见过说书人吗？客人这么问…我想你多半是见过的。
t_en: Have you seen storytellers on other planets? When you put it like that... I can tell you've probably already seen it before.

t_ch: 我只是听说过「说书」是一种深具传统的表演形式，具体也不太了解。不过，老板假借说书人的身份，也许只是方便在茶馆里打听志怪故事而已。
t_en: All I know is, storytelling is a traditional form of performative art. I don't know much else. But perhaps the boss is using his gig as a storyteller to hang out at the teahouse and hear all kinds of supernatural and weird tales.

t_ch: 真希望他能多上点心在书店这里啊…客人，有什么想要买的书吗？
t_en: How I wish he could pay a little more attention to his bookstore business... Ah well. Have you got any books there that you'd like to buy?

t_ch: 我想买本书。
t_en: I'd like to buy a book.

t_ch: 嗯，买书吗？需要什么，我帮你找找。
t_en: Mmm, buying a book? Which one do you need. I'll help you find it.

t_ch: 想和你再随便聊聊。
t_en: I'd like to chat with you some more.

t_ch: 行啊，反正客人是看准了我现在无所事事，那就随便聊聊吧。
t_en: Okay. Well, as you can see, I've nothing to do, so let's chat.

t_ch: 关于仙舟上的志怪故事…
t_en: About supernatural tales aboard the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 书肆老板沉迷于仙舟上的志怪故事，甚至会花费重金去别的仙舟上实地勘察，虽然结果一无所获，但是他本人倒是乐在其中。
t_en: The boss here is obsessed with all the supernatural stories told on this ship, and even pays through the nose to make visits to other Xianzhou ships. He never really finds anything, but he has fun along the way.

t_ch: 实际上，这些所谓的志怪故事大多是来自异星生物、或是平平无奇的巧合，仙舟人见怪不怪，根本不值得大费周章。
t_en: In fact, most of these stories are just due to otherworldly species or mere coincidence. The Xianzhou people aren't really flustered by any of that, and barely raise an eyebrow.

t_ch: 不过老板说过，无论是什么光怪陆离的世界，人们总是需要故弄玄虚的故事。
t_en: But the boss says that no matter how brilliant this world is, people always need to have dark and ghostly stories.

t_ch: 「真实的不太有趣，有趣的不太真实。添油加醋是常事，不清不楚有味道」。这就是他写在腰封上的话。
t_en: "Reality isn't interesting, and what's interesting isn't real. Adding flourish is normal, and vagueness adds flavor." He's got this phrase etched onto his belt.

t_ch: 关于最近的畅销书…
t_en: About recent bestsellers...

t_ch: 最近有本推理小说很火热，但是我没看过，自然没法向你仔细介绍。不过，你要是对内容感兴趣的话，买了就知道了。
t_en: There's a new suspense novel out that's very popular, but I haven't read it yet, so can't go over the exact details. But if you're interested in such stories, just buy it and you'll know for sure.

t_ch: 仙舟上有长生不老的人、有新奇的科技，还有异星的奇人异士…但是大家就喜欢看暴风雪山庄里没有云骑军、没有科技、没有超能力的原始作案手段。
t_en: On Xianzhou, we have immortal people, novel technology, and fantastical visitors from exoplanets... But somehow everyone wants to read about primitive crimes conducted in snowbound manor houses with no Cloud Knights, no technology, and no super powers...

t_ch: 我不是很理解，可能这就是推理的魅力吧？
t_en: I don't really get it. Perhaps that's the charm of mystery novels?

t_ch: 仙舟的书样式还挺复古…
t_en: The appearance of books on Xianzhou all seem kind of retro...

t_ch: 刚到仙舟的游客经常发出这种感慨。
t_en: Ah yes, tourists often make this remark.

t_ch: 其实客人要是感兴趣，就将那些书里对应「书签」取下，靠近自己的玉兆，就能下载阅读了。
t_en: In fact, if you want, you can take the corresponding "bookmark" you like from the books, hold them close to your jade abacus, and download them to read later.

t_ch: 我自己是习惯将书录入玉兆里。但总架不住有人喜欢「手捧书卷的感觉」。活得越久，人就越喜欢追求所谓的仪式感。
t_en: I personally like downloading and reading off my jade abacus, but there's always people who like the feeling of holding paper books in their hands. The longer people live, the more they crave this kind of ceremony.

t_ch: 啧，这些实体书可麻烦了，占着地方，还得每日清理。
t_en: Tsk, these physical books are such a pain. They take up space and need daily cleaning.

t_ch: 我下次再来光临。
t_en: I'll be back next time.

t_ch: 好的，恕不远送。
t_en: Away you go, then. Take care.

t_ch: 客人付了钱，把感兴趣的「书签」取下，靠近玉兆，就能下载阅读了。
t_en: You pay, then pick out the "bookmark" you like, hold them close to your jade abacus, and download them to read later.

t_ch: 要是没有玉兆，又或者喜欢捧书的手感，店里的书您随便取用。卷轴式、简牍式、开本式一应俱全。总之，要买书的时候再来找我吧。
t_en: If you don't have a jade abacus or if you just like the feeling of a book in your hands, just grab one off the shelf. Scroll rolls, bound bamboo strips, printed and bound paper books — we've got all the formats. Anyway, just come see me if you'd like to buy a book.

t_ch: 建木又长起来了…
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor is growing again...
*sigh*

t_ch: 怎么唉声叹气的？
t_en: What's with all the sighing?

t_ch: 你是今天第二十七个问这个问题的客人。
t_en: You are the twenty-seventh person to mention this to me today.

t_ch: 看见了，看见了…要是西衍先生，恐怕会对此感兴趣吧。
t_en: I see, I see... Mr. Xiyan would probably be interested in this.

t_ch: 但我不过是一介看店小童，只希望这东西别妨碍我做生意就好……
t_en: But I'm just a kid helping out here. Hope this thing doesn't impact my work...

t_ch: 建木风波总算平息了…
t_en: Finally, the Ambrosial Arbor crisis is over... What's your take?

t_ch: 不要催啦不要催啦！
t_en: Alright! Alright! Just stop with all the Ambrosial Arbor stuff!

t_ch: 你这不是答非所问吗…
t_en: You didn't answer my question...

t_ch: 催、催什么？
t_en: Wh—What's the rush?

t_ch: 今天有五十二个人来跟我说这事，其中有四十八个问我是否有探讨建木生长与停息之秘的书籍到货。
t_en: There have been <unbreak>52</unbreak> people who came to talk to me about this matter. Among them, <unbreak>48</unbreak> asked me whether there were readables that discussed the secrets behind the growth and ceasing of the Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 我不过是一介看店小童，写书出书的事我哪能决定…哎…要问就问那些编辑去吧，反正我这里还没到货呢。
t_en: As a mere bookstore assistant, I have no say in the writing and publishing of readables... Alas... They should pester those editors instead. Anyway, I have no stock of those readables here.

t_ch: 忽如柳叶随风起，超级无敌我爱你。
t_en: Like willow leaves moving at a speed of knots, super duper love you lots.

t_ch: 秋风明月登楼台，谈恋爱要慢慢来。
t_en: Autumn winds and moon rises on buildings glow, love can also move as slow.

t_ch: …这写诗谁不会啊，她怎么就看不上我呢？我差在哪儿啊？
t_en: ...Writing poetry is so easy. Why doesn't she like me? What am I missing?

t_ch: 换了我也看不上你。
t_en: I wouldn't like you either.

t_ch: 全是情感啊，你没听出来吗？我感觉自己和仙舟七诗圣五五开了啊！
t_en: It's pure emotion. Can't you hear that? I feel like I'm on equal grounds with the seven sacred poets of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 你这诗也太烂了…
t_en: This poem of yours is terrible...

t_ch: 这几句诗不挺好…？「起」、「你」…你看，我都押上韵脚了。七言绝句了属于是。
t_en: You don't like it...? Look, "knots" and "lots"... it all rhymes... four lines of heptameter.

t_ch: …哎，算了，我也不跟你嘴硬了。说实话，我倒现在都没理解诗这东西到底哪里有意思，也没什么分辨好坏的本事。
t_en: ...Ah, forget about it. I'm not arguing with you. To be honest, I don't even really get what's so good about poetry, and can't really tell what's good poetry.

t_ch: 看上去你对诗句有一些独到的见解，你说说看，别人写得情真意切，我写得也是真情实感，我到底差在哪儿了？
t_en: You, however, seem to have unique insights into poetry. Tell me, other people pour out their love and emotion, and I'm here writing out my true feelings too. What am I doing wrong?

t_ch: 可能是差一些文化品味…
t_en: It's missing the sense of culture and taste...

t_ch: 「文化品味」又是什么？你不要发明新词语来敷衍我呀。
t_en: Culture? Taste? What's that? Don't just invent new words to trick me.

t_ch: …可能你确实没有敷衍我。唉，真的就像你说的，我差一些「文化品味」。
t_en: ...Maybe you aren't tricking me. *sigh* You're right. I'm lacking in culture and taste.

t_ch: 其实你写得很有风格！
t_en: I actually think you write with your own style!

t_ch: 很有风格…谢谢你啊。虽然我分不清楚这究竟是夸奖，还是安慰。
t_en: My own style... Thank you. Even though I can't really tell if this is meant as a compliment or consolation.

t_ch: 不过说到底，就算你喜欢，那个人不喜欢，这也没用啊……
t_en: But in the end, even if you do like it, she doesn't like it. So it's useless...

t_ch: 哎…说了半天，你是有什么事吗？
t_en: *sigh* ...Anyways, do you need anything?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊…
t_en: I want to chat with you...

t_ch: 好啊，反正我也没什么灵感…你想找我打听什么，工造技术之类的吗？
t_en: Alright. It's not like I have any inspiration, is it... What are you after? Is it about artifex technology?

t_ch: 你还会工造技术？
t_en: You know artifex technology?

t_ch: 我是工造司的工正，就挺有水平的吧。领导对我很赞赏，他说过「小聪啊，你要是稍微有点情商，百年内就能评上大工正」。
t_en: Yeah, I'm an artisan at the Artisanship Commission, and I'm kind of a big deal. My boss loves me and he said, "Cong, if you just work on your EQ a little, you'll be a Master Artisan in another hundred years."

t_ch: 但说真的，他这话我不爱听。什么年代了，还有这种对理工学者「高智商低情商」的刻板印象。
t_en: But honestly, I hate hearing that. In this day and era, why are there still stereotyped notions that engineers have high IQ and low EQ?

t_ch: 哎…但说这些都是白扯。要是我写诗的技术能赶上我工造技术水平的一半就好了。我现在就不会这么愁了。
t_en: Ack... But that's all a load of bull. If only my poetry were half as good as my artifex skills, I wouldn't be so anxious right now.

t_ch: 人各有所长嘛。
t_en: Everyone's got their own strengths.

t_ch: 话是这么说，但是解决不了问题。哎……
t_en: That may be true, but it doesn't fix my problem. *sigh*...

t_ch: 工造技术也能用于写诗。
t_en: Artifex skills can also be used to write poetry.

t_ch: …实不相瞒，我正有此意，正在想办法呢。
t_en: ...To tell you the truth, that's what I think, too. I'm trying to find a way to do just that.

t_ch: 你为什么执着于写诗？
t_en: Why are you so obsessed with writing poetry?

t_ch: 还不是为了那个人…我们还不太熟，也没必要和你说太细。
t_en: Well, it's not for her... I don't really know you, so I don't want to say too much.

t_ch: 总之呢，我喜欢一个姑娘。但她不喜欢我这样的男人…她喜欢的男人，能用奇怪的韵律将一些奇怪词汇组合在一起，然后摇头晃脑地吟诵出来……
t_en: Anyway, I like this girl. But she doesn't like guys like me... The guys she likes use strange melodies to string together all these strange words, and they just recite them all from memory...

t_ch: 简而言之…那个姑娘喜欢诗人。然而如你所见，我在诗歌上的造诣还略有欠缺。
t_en: In short... this girl loves poets. However, as you can see, I'm slightly lacking when it comes to the poetry department.

t_ch: 你还有没有别的文学作品？
t_en: Have you tried any other forms of literature?

t_ch: 其实我也试了一下仙舟词牌…你听一下我这个……
t_en: Yeah, I tried some Xianzhou sonnets... What do you think about this one?

t_ch: 「山横远翠疑歌黛，彦卿长得没我帅。你好坏，我被你打败，小可爱。」
t_en: "Emerald mounts hold a love fledgling, but I'm way more handsome than Yanqing. So annoying, yet you make my heart sing, you pretty thing."

t_ch: 我个人觉得词比诗更适合直抒胸臆，成品效果比写诗好很多。你觉得还行吗？
t_en: I feel like sonnets allow me to express myself more freely, and produce a more polished result than poetry. What do you think?

t_ch: 写得很不错啊。
t_en: Beautifully written.

t_ch: 说明咱俩品味比较接近。我给我们领导看，他建议我「把这张纸撕碎了咽下去，永远都不要给别人看」。
t_en: That means we have similar taste. I showed it to my boss, but he just told me to shred it up, swallow it, and never show it to anyone ever again.

t_ch: 他好奇怪啊，我们工造司里明明有碎纸的机器。
t_en: He's so weird. We already have a shredder in the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: …这里的「彦卿」非常唐突。
t_en: ...this word, "Yanqing," seems a little abrupt.

t_ch: 你这啥理解能力啊，还在这儿鉴赏诗词呢？
t_en: You have such a bad understanding of poetry, and you're trying to appraise poems?

t_ch: 这里提到「彦卿」，是一种叫作「对比」的修饰手法。这是在说我比那个天才剑客还要帅气、还要潇洒、还要优雅……
t_en: I mention "Yanqing" here as a literary device. I'm saying here that I'm even more handsome, dashing, and graceful than the genius fighter...

t_ch: 行吗？我怎么觉得有点恶心呢？
t_en: Yeah? Why do I feel slightly disgusted?

t_ch: 有点受打击，但是完全能接受。谢谢你的坦诚。
t_en: I feel attacked, but totally acceptable. Thank you for your honesty.

t_ch: 写诗可能还是需要一些素养。
t_en: I think writing poetry requires some training.

t_ch: 我知道你说的是什么意思。这和工造技术是一样的，诗歌存在着「规范」和「技术」，只要我解决了这些难题，就能达到你说的「素养」。
t_en: I know what you mean. This is just the same as developing new artifex tech. Poetry has its rules and techniques. As long as I can solve these problems, then I'll have achieved that level of training.

t_ch: 「规范」说的是仙舟诗依托于语言的特殊韵律，只要将符合韵律的排列组合全部列出来，就能确定「规范」的边界。
t_en: The rules refer to the special rhythm upon which Xianzhou poetry relies. As long as all the different permutations that conform to the rhythm can be exhaustively listed out, then we can define the limits of this rule.

t_ch: 其实「规范」非常简单，但「技术」特别难。无论我去询问谁，他们都说「诗歌不能谈技术，这不浪漫」，可是技术也可以浪漫呀？
t_en: These rules are a piece of cake, and it's the techniques that're particularly hard. But no matter who I ask, they always come back to me with "you can't talk about technique with poetry. That's not romantic." But can't technology be romantic, too?

t_ch: 比如这句：「棍上金工鸿利摇，白纸飞段救鹅毛」…就是我用机器随机组合中最像人话的一句诗，虽然我也没看懂，但不妨碍我能品出其中的技术优雅感？
t_en: For example: "On the staff the goldsmith waves large profit obese, white paper flies stages to rescue the goose"... This is the most humanistic verse I've strung together out of the automated word-combination logarithms. I don't understand what it's trying to say, but can't you sense the elegance of technology here?

t_ch: 对于工造司的工正来说，失败只是成功的必经之路。我准备之后再试试别的办法。
t_en: As for artisans at the Artisanship Commission, failure is a required step on the road to success. I'll try some other things out once I'm prepared.

t_ch: 在想出更好的办法之前，还是要自己试着写写诗啊…哎，写诗真的好难。
t_en: I'll have to keep trying to write poetry on my own until I come up with a better way... *sigh* writing poems sure is difficult.

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 再见，有空再聊。
t_en: So long. Catch you again later.

t_ch: 清风明月…捅我心？清风明月…踹我心？清风明月…掐我心？
t_en: The breeze and the moon... "poke" my heart? The breeze and the moon... "stomp" my heart? The breeze and the moon... "choke" my heart?

t_ch: 到底该用哪个字啊…今天找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Which word to pick... How can I help you today?

t_ch: 建木又长起来了…
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor is growing again...

t_ch: 我为此赋了一首诗，你听完我给你吟一下啊……
t_en: I composed a poem for this. Listen up. I'll chant it for you...

t_ch: 咳咳…鳞渊境里浪滔滔，一棵大树高又高。你一口来我一口，长得像根大串烧。
t_en: Ahem... Scalegorge waters surge and flow; mighty tree you grow and grow; a sip for you, a sip for me; growing like a kebab tree.

t_ch: 怎么样？喜欢吗？…呃，看你表情是不太喜欢…算了，我再想想。
t_en: Huh? Do you like it? ...Uh, I can tell by your expression that you're not a fan... Forget it, I'll come up with another.

t_ch: 你是世界尽头的叹息，而我只是一枚小小的螺丝钉。我手足无措，不知道如何修补你的心。哎，静斋……
t_en: You're a sorrowful sigh at the end of the world, while I am just an insignificant screw. I'm at a loss, helpless in ways to mend your broken heart. Oh, Jingzhai...

t_ch: 噢，你来了，找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Oh, you're here. What brings you here?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊…
t_en: I want to chat with you...

t_ch: 好啊，反正我也没什么灵感…你想找我打听什么，工造技术之类的吗？
t_en: Alright. It's not like I have any inspiration... What are you after? Is it about artifex technology?

t_ch: 你会工造技术？
t_en: You know about artifex technology?

t_ch: 我是工造司的工正，就挺有水平的吧。领导对我很赞赏，他说过「小聪啊，你要是稍微有点情商，百年内就能评上大工正」。
t_en: Yeah, I'm an artisan at the Artisanship Commission, and I'm kind of a big deal. My boss loves me and he said, "Cong, if you just work on your EQ a little, you'll be a Master Artisan in another hundred years."

t_ch: 但说真的，他可能说得没错，我情商确实不太高，哎…不过，我听他们说最近我的情商有所进步，如果不是挖苦我的话，就说明我真的成长了。
t_en: Frankly speaking, I think he's right, as I really don't have a high EQ... But, I've heard them say that I've gotten better lately. If it's not sarcasm, it probably means I have improved.

t_ch: 你现在还写诗吗？
t_en: Do you still write poems now?

t_ch: 写的，不过这次我不再依赖诗仙机器人了，我想自己写一些。
t_en: I do, but I no longer rely on the Poetic Genius Ingenia. I prefer to write the poems myself.

t_ch: 我发现写诗没有想象中那样严肃…我好像爱上写诗了。
t_en: It turns out that writing poems isn't as serious a business as I used to think... I've fallen in love with it.

t_ch: 诗仙机器人们怎么了？
t_en: What about those Poetic Genius Ingenia?

t_ch: 我问了它们各自的想法，让它们去做自己想做的事去了。
t_en: I talked to them about their thoughts and left them to pursue what they desired.

t_ch: 忧郁诗人有些怪，它说适度忧郁感觉更有安全感，让我帮它再调整了一下。有机会的话，你还是能找到它。
t_en: The Gloomy Poet is a quirky one. It told me that the right amount of melancholy makes it feel safe, so it asked me to adjust its settings. You can still bump into it if you're lucky.

t_ch: 忧郁到底是什么呢？忧郁的时候，饱受折磨；不忧郁的时候，又会怀念它。
t_en: What exactly is melancholy then, you might ask? When you're experiencing the emotion, you'll be in agony. When you aren't, you'll miss it.

t_ch: 那么静斋…
t_en: What about Jingzhai...

t_ch: 我知道静斋的心意，我不会再去叨扰她。
t_en: I know what she thinks, and I will leave her alone.

t_ch: 但是我要彻底说服自己不再追求她，还是需要很长的时间，懂的都懂。
t_en: However, it'll take a long time before I can convince myself to give up on her. Anyone who's been through this would understand.

t_ch: 放心，我不会耽误工造司的工作的。
t_en: Don't worry. I won't neglect my job at the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 再见，有空再聊。
t_en: So long. Catch you again later.

t_ch: 建木不再生发了…
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor no longer sprouts...

t_ch: 是啊，危机总算过去了。对了，我最近从建木危机一事上得到了一些灵感，写了几句诗，你听听——
t_en: Indeed, everything is finally over. By the way, I have written a poem with inspiration from the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis. Please listen...

t_ch: 树生树长无定数，似我发丝全干枯。呜呼！莫不是水土不服！
t_en: Nothing is certain when it comes to the growth of the tree, just like the dry hair strands of me. Alas, the environment must be to blame!

t_ch: 我感觉写得还蛮好的…呃，你的表情是怎么回事……
t_en: I think it's quite well-written... Um, what's with your expression...

t_ch: 你好。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 能请你往旁边挪一挪吗？
t_en: Could you please move over a tad?

t_ch: 谁在说话？
t_en: Who's talking?

t_ch: 是我。
t_en: It's me.

t_ch: 你是不是也觉得蘑菇不该说话？
t_en: Do you also think mushrooms shouldn't talk?

t_ch: 可以，但为什么？
t_en: I can... but why?

t_ch: 因为我是一朵正在收集阳光的蘑菇。
t_en: Because I'm a sunbathing mushroom.

t_ch: 可你把它们全挡住了。
t_en: But you blocked off the light.

t_ch: 你是不是也觉得蘑菇不该收集阳光？
t_en: Do you also think mushrooms shouldn't sunbathe?

t_ch: 噢。我好悲伤。
t_en: Oh, I'm so sad.

t_ch: 对不起，我无意冒犯…
t_en: Sorry, I didn't mean to offend...

t_ch: 没关系。
t_en: That's alright.

t_ch: 根本没有人会在乎一朵蘑菇的感受。
t_en: Nobody cares about how a mushroom feels.

t_ch: 可你明明只是一把伞。
t_en: But you're an umbrella.

t_ch: 所有人都这么说。
t_en: That's what everyone says.

t_ch: 人们都说我是「工造司民用维修单元甲型」。
t_en: People say I'm an "Artisanship Commission's Civil Maintenance Unit Type-A."

t_ch: 可我只是一朵蘑菇。
t_en: But I'm a mushroom.

t_ch: 噢。我更悲伤了。
t_en: Oh, now I feel even sadder.

t_ch: 我很好奇…
t_en: I'm curious...

t_ch: 我会知无不言。
t_en: I'll tell you everything I know.

t_ch: 尽管我只是朵蘑菇。
t_en: Even though I'm just a mushroom.

t_ch: 你到底是什么？
t_en: What the heck are you?

t_ch: 我会说自己是蘑菇。
t_en: I'd call myself a mushroom.

t_ch: 你会说我是什么？
t_en: What would you say I am?

t_ch: 好吧，你就是一朵蘑菇。
t_en: Fine then. You're a mushroom.

t_ch: 真的吗？
t_en: Really?

t_ch: 可你听起来很不情愿。
t_en: But you sound reluctant.

t_ch: 还是觉得你像把伞…
t_en: You still look like an umbrella to me...

t_ch: 蘑菇当然长得像一把伞。
t_en: Of course, Mushrooms are shaped like an umbrella.

t_ch: 我想你也对蘑菇有些偏见。
t_en: I think you're a little biased against mushrooms.

t_ch: 你有没有可能是个机器人？
t_en: Could you actually be a robot?

t_ch: 有可能。
t_en: Possible.

t_ch: 或许我是一朵机械蘑菇。
t_en: Maybe I'm a robot mushroom.

t_ch: 那我岂不是血统不纯？
t_en: Then wouldn't that mean I'm not pure-blooded?

t_ch: 噢。我好悲伤。
t_en: Oh, I'm so sad.

t_ch: 你为什么在这里？
t_en: Why are you here?

t_ch: 因为我是一朵失败的蘑菇。
t_en: Because I'm a failure of a mushroom.

t_ch: 怎么就失败了？
t_en: How did you fail?

t_ch: 可你是一朵蘑菇…
t_en: But you're a mushroom...

t_ch: 你是不是也觉得蘑菇不该失败？
t_en: Do you also think mushrooms shouldn't fail?

t_ch: 唉。我习惯了。
t_en: Ugh, I'm used to it.

t_ch: 其实我曾有一位主人。
t_en: Actually, I used to have a master.

t_ch: 他是一名工造司的巧匠。
t_en: He was a skilled craftsman at the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 主人想让我帮他作诗。
t_en: My master wants me to help him write poetry.

t_ch: 我说：「可我只是一朵蘑菇。」
t_en: I said, "but I'm just a mushroom."

t_ch: 所以你失败了。
t_en: And so you failed.

t_ch: 是的。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 我只不过是朵不会作诗的蘑菇。
t_en: I'm just a mushroom who can't write poems.

t_ch: 这难道不就是诗吗？
t_en: Isn't that poetry?

t_ch: 什么是诗？
t_en: What is poetry?

t_ch: 其实我根本不明白。
t_en: I don't understand the first thing about it.

t_ch: 因为我只是一朵蘑菇。
t_en: Because I'm a mushroom.

t_ch: 悲伤淹没了你…
t_en: Sadness overwhelms you...

t_ch: 你已经学会抢答了。
t_en: You've learned how to give quick answers.

t_ch: 噢。我好悲伤。
t_en: Oh, I'm so sad.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 愿你的前路长满蘑菇。
t_en: May your path be filled with many mushrooms.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Bye.

t_ch: 再见，蘑菇。
t_en: So long, mushroom.

t_ch: 你平时也会向蘑菇搭话吗？
t_en: Do you often talk to mushrooms?

t_ch: 噢。我好悲伤。
t_en: Oh, I'm so sad.

t_ch: 你注意到建木又长起来了吗？
t_en: Did you notice the Ambrosial Arbor was growing once again?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 大约因为我只是一个蘑菇。
t_en: It's probably because I'm just a mushroom.

t_ch: 蘑菇只关心大地，不去看高远的东西。
t_en: Mushrooms only care about the ground and not things way up at that kind of altitude.

t_ch: 建木停止生发的消息你听说了吗？
t_en: Have you heard that the Ambrosial Arbor has stopped sprouting?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 蘑菇没听说，蘑菇不在意。
t_en: I've not heard about it and do not care.

t_ch: 蘑菇只想转起来，像个旋风小蘑菇。
t_en: I just want to start spinning like a little tornado.

t_ch: …如果每个人都像你这么想，托蝶幻境这门艺术就要断绝了！如果当年子润老师听了制片人的这些所谓「商业考量」，《外婆桥》根本都不会问世！
t_en: ...If everyone thought like you did, the art of Butterfly Immersia would be wiped out! If master Zi Run had listened to these so-called "business interests" back then, "The Bridge to Grandma's Home" would've never even been released!

t_ch: 呵，那你就和你的艺术梦想一起去桥洞子底下喝西北风去吧。你当我是为了害你才和你说这些的吗？大家一起赚钱不好吗，你为什么这么犟呢……
t_en: Ha, then you can take your artistic dreams and your bridge — and jump off it into a lake! Do you think I said this to cause you pain? Why can't we all make a little money together? Why have you gotta be so pig-headed...

t_ch: 可是…艺术是不能单纯用商业衡量的啊！如果你根本就不尊重艺术，又为什么要在这个行业里以此谋生呢？
t_en: But... Art cannot be measured by commercial impact alone! Why would you try to make a living in this industry if you have no respect for art at all?

t_ch: 打起来！打起来！
t_en: Fight! Fight!

t_ch: …你又是什么人呢？拱火我们俩吵架，你又是什么意思。
t_en: ...And who are you? Egging us on to fight like this... What can you get out of this?

t_ch: 哎…武赫，消停点吧。别让人看热闹了。
t_en: *sigh* Wu He, calm down. Let's not put on a show for people.

t_ch: 你们俩不要再吵啦！
t_en: You two: Quit arguing!

t_ch: 哎呀，我这…确实不好意思，我们俩是有点儿上头，没注意到周围。
t_en: Oh, I... I'm really sorry about that. We kind of went a bit goblin mode there and should've paid more attention to our surroundings.

t_ch: 哎…武赫，消停点吧。别让人看热闹了。
t_en: *sigh* Wu He, calm down. Let's not put on a show for people.

t_ch: 你们在这等着，我去找下地衡司。
t_en: You guys wait here. I'll go find the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 别别别，我们这点儿小打小闹就不麻烦地衡司了。
t_en: No no no, please don't bother the Realm-Keeping Commission with this little tiff of ours.

t_ch: 哎…武赫，消停点吧。别让人看热闹了。
t_en: *sigh* Wu He, calm down. Let's not put on a show for people.

t_ch: 我和你闹，那还是不是因为艺术创作嘛…那个{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}我来解释一下，我们俩是搞托蝶幻境的，她是制片人南洁，我是导演武赫。
t_en: I'm arguing with you out of creative license, right... Let me explain to the {F#girl}{M#boy} here — we're Butterfly Immersia creatives. She's Nanjie, the producer — and I'm the director, Wu He.

t_ch: 就这样打住吧，正好你也冷静冷静。不好意思，让你见笑了。是有什么事情要说吗？
t_en: Let's stop there, for you to get calmed down. Sorry to make you laugh. Have you got anything you'd like to say?

t_ch: 想要随便聊聊…
t_en: Just a casual chat...

t_ch: 难不成是你的影迷，好啊，武赫你多来讲讲。
t_en: Is it possible that you've got a fan here? Alright, keep talking, Wu He.

t_ch: 托蝶幻境是什么？
t_en: What's Butterfly Immersia all about?

t_ch: 托蝶幻境是一种仙舟特有的艺术形式，使用流传数千年狐族幻术与当代工造技术相结合，可以创造出以假乱真的幻境，让观众全方位沉浸在情景之中。
t_en: Butterfly Immersia is a unique art form found in Xianzhou. It combines ancient foxian illusions with modern artifex tech to create an incredibly real dreamland that completely immerses the audience right in the middle of the scene.

t_ch: 哇，听起来好厉害…
t_en: Wow, sounds pretty awesome...

t_ch: 其实就是你们的虚拟现实技术罢了。
t_en: Actually, it's all just virtual reality tech.

t_ch: 这不就是虚拟现实吗…
t_en: Isn't this virtual reality...

t_ch: 此言差矣！托蝶幻境是仙舟独特的文化和艺术，并不是什么……
t_en: Wrong! Butterfly Immersia is a unique cultural legacy and art form found only on Xianzhou, not some crazy...

t_ch: 别说那么复杂，本质就是虚拟现实技术。
t_en: Don't make it so complicated. The essence is basically virtual reality.

t_ch: 托蝶幻境和「虚拟现实」能一样吗？幻戏的核心在于「狐术」的应用！
t_en: How can Butterfly Immersia be the same as VR? Its core revolves around a base of foxian magic!

t_ch: 充分调动五感，模糊现实和虚拟，享受纯粹的艺术时空，主观视角与客观视角的交替……
t_en: It fully engrosses the five senses and blurs reality with illusion creating a purely artistic time in space: The alternation of the subjective with the objective...

t_ch: 所以具体有什么区别…
t_en: So what exactly is the difference, then...

t_ch: 具体…区别…区别就是…这是文化！这是艺术！是艺术你懂吗！
t_en: The exact... difference... is that... This is culture! This is art! It's art, you know!

t_ch: 没什么区别，就是虚拟现实。
t_en: There's really not much difference. It's basically just VR.

t_ch: 你们是做什么的？
t_en: What do you guys do for a living?

t_ch: 我们隶属于「香薷幻娱」，是罗浮上最大的托蝶幻境制作工坊。我是个制片人，他是个导演。
t_en: We're affiliates of Elsholtzia Fantasy Entertainment, the greatest Butterfly Immersia production studio in all of Xianzhou. I'm the producer, and he's the director.

t_ch: 最大的工坊？星际和平娱乐已经将巨量的资本轰入幻戏界，建立起了「星和幻娱」，「香薷幻娱」已经完蛋了！
t_en: The largest production studio? The IPC has poured vast quantities of capital into the genre, creating their own Interastral Peace Entertainment subsidiary. Elsholtzia Fantasy Entertainment is done for — it's over!

t_ch: 抱歉，他有点情绪激动。…「星和幻娱」只是尝试在仙舟本土试验一种新的娱乐模式——「交感幻戏」，跟我们的传统幻戏不一样。
t_en: Sorry, he gets a bit emotional sometimes. Interastral Peace Entertainment is just trying to test out a new kind of homegrown entertainment format called "Empathic Immersia" — which is different from our traditional performing art.

t_ch: 「交感幻戏」会提供一个有多种故事走向的幻境。
t_en: Empathic Immersia will provide a dreamworld with multiple different story directions.

t_ch: 这个我熟，电子游戏。
t_en: I know this. You mean "video games," right?

t_ch: 反正差不多就是那一套肤浅的东西吧……
t_en: Pretty much, it's trivial pap...

t_ch: 听起来很先进…
t_en: Sounds really advanced...

t_ch: 实际上也的确很先进。
t_en: Actually, it really does use quite advanced tech.

t_ch: 可惜武赫大导演嫌弃人家「交感幻戏不是艺术」。
t_en: It's a pity that our director Wu He is such a snob, saying that "Empathic Immersia is not a legitimate art form."

t_ch: 交感幻戏现在不是艺术，是因为还缺乏一个真正的艺术家。如果不是让公司那些目光短浅的人瞎搞，而是我来操刀，也许就能让「交感幻戏」成为真正的艺术……
t_en: Empathic Immersia cannot be an art form, because it lacks a true artist in its ranks. If I were contracted to do some Empathic Immersia instead of those suits at the company dilly-dallying, then maybe it could have become a true art form...

t_ch: 所以，现在交感幻戏又是艺术了？
t_en: So let me get this straight: Now Empathic Immersia is a legitimate art form again?

t_ch: 我只是担心也许会有一天，人们不会觉得「托蝶幻境」是仙舟狐人的文化遗产，而是公司的一项新发明…一想到这样的未来，我就觉得浑身战栗。
t_en: I'm just worried that perhaps there'll come a day when people won't remember that Butterfly Immersia is actually a cultural relic of Xianzhou foxians, but instead just think it's this product invented by the IPC... I can't help but shudder when I contemplate such a future...

t_ch: 你们接下来有什么打算？
t_en: So what are your next plans for the future, then?

t_ch: 接下来？继续说服武赫拍一些不会让我们饿死的幻戏。
t_en: Next? Keep on convincing Wu He to shoot a few immersia scenes so that we don't starve to death.

t_ch: 饿不死的，别这么短视。我要认真考虑一下要不要投身「交感幻戏」……
t_en: We're not starving to death. Don't be so short-sighted. I have to give serious consideration as to whether or not I decide to get into this Empathic Immersia thing...

t_ch: 我知道你是在说气话，但是…这未必不是一个方向。依我看，公司格局还是太小了。不断有新内容产出的「托蝶幻境」，不应该止步于娱乐。
t_en: I know you're angry, but... It's not necessarily taking things in a different direction. In my opinion, our company is too small scale. Butterfly Immersia has a constant stream of new content being released, and shouldn't stop at just entertainment.

t_ch: 他们的技术已经可以创造出一个规模庞大的虚拟生活社区，而不是个电子游戏。
t_en: Their technology is already capable of creating a large-scale VR community, rather than just some video game.

t_ch: 你什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 是我的话，我要创造一个十亿仙舟人愿意生活在其中的托蝶幻境！
t_en: If it were me, I'd be hungry to create a Butterfly Immersia in which billions of Xianzhou people would love to live!

t_ch: 你们这些制片，除了钱和人情之外还能不能多接触一点广阔的宇宙？
t_en: You producers... Besides money and favors, what else do you know about the ways of the world?

t_ch: 我说，你有没有想过银河中已经存在类似的东西了。比如说，某个叫朋克洛德的星球？
t_en: I'm telling you, have you ever considered that something like this already exists out there in the cosmos? Like, on a planet called Punklorde?

t_ch: 不打扰你们了。我没别的事了。
t_en: I won't bother you two. I've nothing else to say.

t_ch: 好呀，有时间的话一定要体验一次托蝶幻境。
t_en: Okay. Well, if you ever get the chance, you simply have to experience Butterfly Immersia at least once.

t_ch: …现代传媒将每个具有审美能力的灵魂焚毁、煅烧、熔炼，然后铸造成一条「创意生产线」，再得意洋洋地对我们说：「创意生产线不需要创意」！
t_en: ...Modern media breaks down, pulverizes, and distills every soul capable of aesthetic beauty into a "creative assembly line" — then has the cheek to turn around and tell us "The creative assembly line doesn't need any more creativity"!

t_ch: 可是谁掌握了创造艺术的资源，谁就掌握了对艺术的话语权…嗯？先停一下…你又是有什么事要找我们吗？
t_en: But whoever has the resources to create art also has the right to create art... Uh... Hold on a minute... Are you looking for us? What's up?

t_ch: ……我、我当然知道！但，朋克洛德没有可验证的盈利模式！
t_en: ...I, I... of course I know! But, Punklorde hasn't validated a profitable business model yet!

t_ch: 嗤，因为在那里，接入元域是免费的。
t_en: Pah! Because there, it's free to access the metafield.

t_ch: 你注意到建木又长起来了吗？
t_en: Did you notice the Ambrosial Arbor was growing once again?

t_ch: 注意到了啊…刚才还和南洁讨论来着。建木重新长出来以前，想拍摄相关内容就必须花钱做特效……
t_en: I sure did... I was just chatting with Nanjie about it. Before it started growing, we had to spend budget on visual effects if we wanted to shoot that...

t_ch: 但现在我们可以实拍了，效果又好又能节约成本。
t_en: But now we can shoot the real thing — the effects are brilliant and we also save on our bottom line.

t_ch: 可是这大概要请相关部门允许我们去那边拍幻戏吧…听起来就要扯皮很久。
t_en: However, we'll probably need permits from all the relevant departments before we can shoot immersia scenes there... And we'll probably have to wrangle with that for far too long.

t_ch: 这种向上公关的麻烦事，不就是制片人的工作吗？
t_en: Isn't this public relations hassle exactly the kind of thing a producer does?

t_ch: 合着压力又给到我这边了是吧…不过也好，我确实想在建木上面拍一部武侠幻戏，就叫《决战建木之巅》怎么样？
t_en: Passing all the pressure of this partnership over to me once again... But it's cool. I'm actually keen to shoot a martial arts immersia on top of the Ambrosial Arbor. We can call it "Treetop Death Match: Arbor Awakened." Sounds awesome, right?

t_ch: 你这个艺术品味啊……
t_en: You really have no taste...

t_ch: 建木的事你们听说了吗？
t_en: Have you guys heard about what happened to the Ambrosial Arbor?

t_ch: 你是说建木不再生发的事吗？早就知道了啊，南洁还劝我趁热度在火速拍几部呢。
t_en: Are you talking about the fact that the Ambrosial Arbor no longer sprouts? That's old news to me. Nanjie even advised me to make some immersias about it while it is still a hot topic.

t_ch: 这种机会可不多见，趁大家现在还在念叨这事，可以多编排几部，自带热度还省下不少宣发的费用呢。
t_en: Such a great opportunity is hard to come by! You should make immersias about it when the topic is still the talk of the town. In this way, you don't even need to spend much on promotions.

t_ch: 我是觉得没什么创作的必要，我们一是不知道内情，二是又申请不到实地拍摄的机会，牵强附会的东西真没人爱看。
t_en: I don't think it's a good idea. Firstly, we don't know the inside story. Secondly, we won't have the chance to shoot on-site. No one would like to watch such contrived stuff.

t_ch: 思路打开点，说建木未必要真到建木跟前去拍，可以拍点地衡司录事啊云骑啊他们的视角，搞多点打戏和特效，渲染得紧张些就行了。
t_en: Don't be so rigid. An immersia about the Ambrosial Arbor doesn't have to include scenes of the real tree. We can film it from the perspectives of the clerks and Cloud Knights from the Realm-Keeping Commission. Just throw in some action scenes and special effects to create tension, and the immersia will do fine.

t_ch: 那这顶多算《建木风云：前传》，故事都没讲完呢。
t_en: If that's the case, the title of the immersia should be Legend of Ambrosial Arbor: Prequel, since the story is only told halfway.

t_ch: 接下来可以再出一部《密探零零幺》，就说神策府派了一位高手前去调查建木，路上却遇到了几位不着调的人……
t_en: Next, we can release one titled Agent 001 about an agent from the Seat of Divine Foresight who runs into some troublemakers on his way to investigate the Ambrosial Arbor...

t_ch: 闹出了些不大不小的笑话，最后发现建木的事本身是一场乌龙。整一个合家欢，大家都爱看。
t_en: Some funny incidents take place along the way. In the end, everyone realizes the Ambrosial Arbor crisis is nothing but a misunderstanding. It will be a comedy and a crowd-pleaser.

t_ch: …算了，你压根就不懂幻戏。我就不该指望你的……
t_en: ...Forget it. You know nothing about immersias...

t_ch: 这位客人，要买点什么吗…嗯？看您的穿着打扮，既不像囚犯也不像狱卒…稀客啊！
t_en: Would you like to buy something, dear customer...? From the way you dress, you don't seem to be a prisoner or a guard... A rare sight, indeed!

t_ch: 客人？这称呼是从何说起啊…
t_en: A customer? In this place...?

t_ch: 哈哈，看来您确实不太了解我们集团。在这幽囚狱中，人人都是我的客人！
t_en: Haha, looks like you're not familiar with our group. In The Shackling Prison, everyone's my customer!

t_ch: 怎么幽囚狱里还有人开店？
t_en: Someone opened a shop in The Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 哈哈，看来您确实不太了解我们集团。犯人也是人啊，有人的地方就有需求，有需求的地方就有生意。我们集团的使命就是让幽囚狱没有难做的生意。
t_en: Haha, looks like you're not familiar with our group. Prisoners are people too. Where there are people, there would be needs, and needs drive business. Our group's mission is to ensure you can do every sort of business in The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 忘了自我介绍了，我是阿合马铁窗集团首席执行官——阿合马。您也可以叫我马老板或者老马。
t_en: Apologies, I forgot to introduce myself. I'm Arma, chief executive of the Arma Correctional Group. You can also call me boss or shopkeep.

t_ch: 我们集团在幽囚狱经营多年，生产了多款畅销全狱的爆款产品，比如这瓶「铁窗牌沐浴露」，不仅有除垢杀菌的功效，还能保持全身的凉爽舒适......
t_en: Our group has been operating in The Shackling Prison for many years. We've produced many bestsellers that are popular throughout the prison, such as this Arma Correctional Body Wash. Not only does it remove grime and kill germs, but it also leaves the body feeling cool and refreshed...

t_ch: 不过，看您这打扮，应该是其他世界派来幽囚狱视察访问的贵客吧？
t_en: But, looking at how you're dressed, you must be a distinguished guest from another world sent here to inspect The Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 像您这样的贵客，八成看不上我们集团这些粗制滥造的产品…但监狱里由囚犯们满怀匠心制作的产品，您在其他世界可见不着！
t_en: A guest with your esteem probably won't like our group's shoddy products... But we do have products crafted with our prisoners' full ingenuity. You won't find these anywhere else!

t_ch: 不如随便买点什么吧！无论作为旅行纪念品，还是带回家作为伴手礼，都绝对倍儿有面子！
t_en: Why not buy something as a souvenir for yourself or a family member? I guarantee it'll be something that raises your prestige back home!

t_ch: 哦对了，我们不收现金——毕竟收了我也用不了。幽囚狱里的硬通货是「奇巧零食」，您拿这个跟我交换就好。
t_en: By the way, we don't accept cash here. We can't spend them anyway. The common currency in The Shackling Prison is Trick Snacks, so you can use that to buy things from me.

t_ch: 怎么样？阿合马铁窗集团有什么能为您服务的吗？
t_en: How is it? How can the Arma Correctional Group be of service?

t_ch: 给我看看货吧…
t_en: Show me the goods...

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 请您尽管问吧！为客人答疑解惑也是我应尽的义务。
t_en: Ask anything you like! Answering customers' questions is also part of my duties.

t_ch: 你犯了什么罪？
t_en: What crime did you commit?

t_ch: 哎…步离人还能有什么罪啊？「战争罪」呗…但我事先声明啊！我手上可没有过什么血债！
t_en: *sigh* What else can a borisin be accused of? War crimes! But let me be clear! I don't have any blood on my hands!

t_ch: 我从小就不喜欢打架，只喜欢做买卖…整个猎群都觉得我是个可悲的笑话，我爹也以我为耻。
t_en: I've never enjoyed fighting since young. I only like business... The whole pack thought I was pathetic. Even my father thought I was an embarrassment.

t_ch: 后来有一次，猎群出去劫掠商队时，我爹就把我塞进了兽舰，想让我「手上沾点血，成为一个真正的男人！」
t_en: One day while the pack was out looting a caravan, my father forced me onto a beast ship so I would "stain my hands with blood and become a real man!"

t_ch: 结果我还没来得及做什么坏事呢，那艘兽舰就被击落了…醒来的时候，我已经成了幽囚狱里的战犯，这一关就是几百年。
t_en: Before I could do anything bad, the beast ship was shot down. By the time I woke up, I was already a war criminal in The Shackling Prison. It's been hundreds of years since then.

t_ch: 哎！我若是当初投胎在仙舟，现在高低是个优秀企业家！
t_en: *sigh* If only I had been born on board the Xianzhou! I would be a successful entrepreneur by now!

t_ch: 怎么想到在幽囚狱做生意？
t_en: What made you start doing business in The Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 说来也是机缘巧合，刚进来的时候我也一度迷茫，感觉整个人生就要荒废在这个鬼地方了。那愁的我是整日挠头啊，耳朵上的毛都被我挠秃了。
t_en: It was purely by chance. When I first arrived, I was totally lost. I thought I was going to rot away here for the rest of my life. I would scratch my head in frustration every day until the fur between my ears fell out.

t_ch: 后来，一只机巧鸟飞到了我的牢房前，扑腾着扔下了几包奇巧零食，说要购买我挠下来的毛。
t_en: Then one day, a cycrane flew to my cell and dropped a few packets of Trick Snacks on the floor. It said it wanted to buy the fur I had scratched off.

t_ch: 我问它，要我的毛干什么用？它说步离人毛发特别结实，用它们搓出来的绳子，在这幽囚狱里可是千金难求的好东西。
t_en: I asked what it wanted to do with my fur. It said that borisin fur is known to be durable, and that rope made from borisin fur is a rarity in The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 那一刻我突然意识到，原来万物皆有自己的价值。
t_en: That was when I realized everything in this world has its worth.

t_ch: 不久后，我就和这位白鸟老弟一起成立了「阿合马铁窗集团」，专注于用有限的资源为客户提供无限的服务！
t_en: Not long after, I established the Arma Correctional Group with Shiratori. Our goal is to provide limitless service to our customers when we only have limited resources!

t_ch: 狱卒不管你们吗？
t_en: The prison guards let you do it?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，明面上当然是不允许我们搞这些活动的，但私底下，赤衣大人和我们约法三章，只要听话、守规矩，就默许我们搞一点「小买卖」。
t_en: Heh heh! Of course they can't openly allow us to conduct business, but Madam Chiyi made a private deal with us. As long as we follow the rules and obey instructions, she'll turn a blind eye to our "little venture."

t_ch: 按她的说法：「让你们这些无所事事的囚犯们有点事做，也省得你们惹是生非、打架斗殴。」不过我本来也不喜欢打架啦～
t_en: As she puts it: "Giving bored prisoners something to do will keep your noses clean and prevent you from starting fights." Of course, I didn't enjoy fighting to begin with.

t_ch: 步离人也会做生意？
t_en: Borisin know how to do business?

t_ch: 客人您这话说得，好像我们是一群野人似的。这做生意无非讲究一个低买高卖...哦不，互惠互利，有什么难的？
t_en: You seem to be implying we're uncivilized, dear customer. Doing business is all about buying low and selling high — I mean, mutual profit. It's not that difficult.

t_ch: 虽然我本来就不喜欢打架啦…但即使是猎群里的那些暴力份子，也时不时地要去和其他猎群、丰饶民，甚至泯灭帮交换些资源的。
t_en: I may not enjoy fighting, but even the more violent members of the pack have to go trade resources with other packs' members, other Denizens of Abundance, or the Annihilation Gang.

t_ch: 客人，刻板印象可要不得呀～
t_en: It's not good to believe in stereotypes, dear customer.

t_ch: 你的货都是从哪来的？
t_en: Where do you get your goods?

t_ch: 幽囚狱的情况您也知道，别说从外边进货，就是连个苍蝇都飞不进来。所以我们集团的商品全部是就地取材，纯手工打造。
t_en: I'm sure you're aware of the situation in The Shackling Prison. Even a fly can't make its way inside, so we have to forget about importing goods. All the products from our group are sourced from local materials and handcrafted.

t_ch: 像吃剩的饭、掉下来的牙、甚至是身上搓下来的泥...这些外面人眼里的垃圾，在我这可都是珍贵的原材料。
t_en: Things like leftover food, teeth that fell out, or dirt scraped off someone's body... They may look like trash to an outsider, but they're all precious raw materials to us.

t_ch: 您是不知道，我为了把脱落的毛发编织成绳子，付出了多少时间和心血！这东西要是放在外面，那不得卖个艺术品的价格啊！
t_en: You don't know how much time and effort I've put into weaving my shed hairs into a rope! If I were selling it outside, it would be sold as a piece of art!

t_ch: 不过最近这市场大环境是一天比一天差了！本来我们集团前景一片光明，不仅交易额节节攀升，集团规模也扩大到了三人……
t_en: However, the general market conditions have been on the down lately! Originally, our group had a bright future. Our transaction volume was rising, and we even expanded operations and recruited a third person...

t_ch: 但自从战首越狱后，幽囚狱也加强了管理，集团的销售渠道遭遇了重大打击…赤衣大人让我们最近都消停点，不要在这种时候给十王上眼药。哎，难受。
t_en: But ever since the Warhead escaped from the prison, management of The Shackling Prison has become stricter. Most of the group's sales channels suffered a major blow... Madam Chiyi also instructed us to lie low for now and not cause trouble for the Ten Lords. *sigh* How miserable.

t_ch: 不过我相信只要我们集团内部团结一心，持续为客户提供优质的服务，就肯定能挺过这个寒冬！
t_en: But I believe that as long as our group stays united and continues to provide premium service to our customers, we'll definitely be able to brave through these harsh circumstances!

t_ch: 感觉你也没做什么坏事。
t_en: It feels like you didn't do anything bad.

t_ch: 当初十王司审判我的时候，我也是这么说的啊！可惜判官们说了，无论有何苦衷，参与侵略战争就是错的，必须要为此付出代价。
t_en: I said the exact same thing when I was sentenced by the Ten-Lords Commission! But the judges said that no matter what reasons I had, participating in an invasion was wrong, and I had to pay the price.

t_ch: 但那几位判官还是很讲道理的，人家和我说了，只要我能像镜流大人一样立下「十三奇功」，就能给我安排特殊待遇，每周离开幽囚狱，撂地摆摊一天！
t_en: However, the judges were quite reasonable. They told me that if I could complete "<unbreak>13</unbreak> Outstanding Accolades" like Madam Jingliu, they would grant me special arrangements and allow me to leave The Shackling Prison once every week to set up shop outside!

t_ch: 这饼画得也太过分了…
t_en: Isn't that asking for too much?

t_ch: 你学镜流？真的假的？
t_en: Like Jingliu? Are you serious?

t_ch: 我乍一听也觉得太难了，但判官们给我指了条明路——协助十王司工作1次，就算1次「小功」。完成<unbreak>13</unbreak>次「小功」，可以合成1次「中功」。
t_en: At first, I too thought that it was way too difficult, but the judges gave me a clear direction — each time I help the Ten-Lords Commission, it would be considered 1 "Small Merit." After accumulating <unbreak>13</unbreak> "Small Merits," they can be combined into a "Moderate Merit."

t_ch: 拥有<unbreak>13</unbreak>次「中功」以后，就可以合成1次「大功」；拥有<unbreak>13</unbreak>次「大功」以后，就可以合成1次「奇功」！
t_en: After accumulating <unbreak>13</unbreak> "Moderate Merits," they can then be combined into a "Huge Merit," and after accumulating <unbreak>13</unbreak> "Huge Merits," they can be combined into an "Outstanding Accolade"!

t_ch: 只要攒满了这<unbreak>13</unbreak>次「奇功」，我每周就能离开一次幽囚狱了！唉呀妈呀，越想越激动！太温情了，十王司！
t_en: Once I accumulate <unbreak>13</unbreak> "Outstanding Accolades," I'll be able to leave The Shackling Prison once every week! Darn, I'm feeling excited just thinking about it! The Ten-Lords Commission is truly compassionate!

t_ch: 这是一道数学题啊？
t_en: Is this a math question?
